home.colophon.body.0.afterMono: for the small machine voice. Built with Astro 5, two tiny React islands, and self-hosted woff2. Zero CDN, zero analytics, zero cookies.

home.colophon.body.1: V3 of this site was built by DevSwarm, the five-persona Claude Code orchestrator described in Projects.
home.colophon.body.2: Personal site. Opinions my own. Nothing here is investment advice or a commercial offer.
home.colophon.heading: How this site is made.
home.contact.body: Always interested in conversations about data engineering, automation, applied AI, multi-agent systems, knowledge engineering, agent infrastructure, especially where they meet regulated workflows.
home.cv.blurb: Updated as roles, projects and credentials change. The condensed PDF version is on request.

home.hero.lead: I'm Tom. I build AI-augmented automation for the operational work in regulated finance: regulatory notes, accounts quality review, document workflows, mailbox triage, and the reusable tooling that ties them together. Six years in Luxembourg fund services across PE, Private Debt and Real Estate, now spent designing the systems that do the work instead of doing it by hand. This site is the evidence: a live CV, the patterns behind the work, and case studies of it shipping.

home.now.blurb.pre: A public note of where my head is right now, in the spirit of
home.now.heading: What I'm focused on this quarter.
home.now.items.building.v: DevSwarm, a five-persona Claude Code orchestrator that turns an idea into a working PR. V3 of this site is being built by it.
home.now.items.learning.v: How to design AI skills that other people on the team can actually use without me hovering. And how to make multi-agent orchestration boring enough to ship.
home.now.items.offclock.v: Lifting in the morning. A bit of writing in the evening. Trying to be outside more.
home.now.items.running.lead: A personal exocortex on a Fedora server:
home.now.items.running.middle: (44 docs indexed), Meridian, Notion and Asana fanned out through
home.now.items.running.tail: . Accessed from G2 glasses and a Pixel through Tailscale.
home.now.items.working.v: SPV control, statutory reporting and waterfall mechanics. Payment automation across the SPV book.
home.reading.blurb: Themes I keep coming back to. A proper bookshelf with titles will go here once I've trimmed the list.
home.reading.heading: Mostly non-fiction.
home.reading.themes.0.n: How operational excellence actually gets built and kept. Applies to finance ops as much as to engineering.
home.reading.themes.1.n: Encoding expertise into tools other people can use without becoming experts themselves.
home.reading.themes.2.n: Practical books on what current AI can and cannot do, and where it changes the work.
home.reading.themes.3.n: Continuing the line my degrees started. International economics, capital markets, regulation.
home.work.blurb: Sanitised. Employer name, entity names and figures removed. Notes on how I think about this kind of work in fund services.
home.work.case.01.blurb: A pattern for how a Claude skill can read a trial balance, identify entity type, and generate disclosure notes in a firm's chosen style. Tables, movement schedules, narrative.
home.work.case.01.label: order of magnitude time savings on this pattern
home.work.case.01.title: Automated Regulatory Notes Generation
home.work.case.02.blurb: How I think about pre-audit cross-checking. A pattern that takes annual accounts (PDF) and source reconciliation (Excel), checks every cross-reference between primary statements and notes, and flags inconsistencies before the auditor finds them.
home.work.case.02.label: automated cross-checker
home.work.case.02.title: Annual Accounts Quality Review
home.work.case.03.blurb: A pattern for aggregating raw transactional share-register data into net positions per shareholder per share class, applying rule-based redaction for non-strategic shareholders, and producing print-ready Excel and PDF.
home.work.case.03.label: share registers for KYC/AML reviews
home.work.case.03.title: Confidential Document Anonymisation
home.work.case.04.blurb: A pattern for triaging email and meeting-transcript backlogs into prioritised morning briefings. Surfaces unanswered messages, follow-ups, and half-finished commitments, organised by priority and workstream.
home.work.case.04.label: morning briefing automation
home.work.case.04.title: Cross-Platform Mailbox Intelligence
home.work.case.05.blurb: Patterns for documenting payment and accounting workflows in a way new joiners can self-serve. Payment flow, exception handling, approval routing, recharge and intercompany cases.
home.work.case.05.label: structured payment and accounting guide
home.work.case.05.title: Process Documentation & Knowledge Transfer
home.work.case.06.blurb: Patterns I've worked through during fund-accounting platform rollouts. Pref-share reconciliation logic, waterfall validation approaches, edge-case discovery.
home.work.case.06.label: contribution to platform rollout
home.work.case.06.title: Fund Accounting Platform Integration
home.work.case.07.blurb: A library of small, opinionated AI skill patterns. Notes generation, quality review, anonymisation, mailbox triage, classification. Each with deterministic inputs and outputs. From single-purpose skills to five-persona orchestration, see Projects.
home.work.case.07.label: of reusable Claude skill patterns
home.work.case.07.title: AI Skill Architecture & Reusable Tooling
home.work.heading: Patterns I've worked through.
home.workflow.blurb: Point AI at the manual workflows underneath a finance desk. It surfaces what is unbooked, mismatched, or missing, builds the upload template, and drafts the counterparty email. The operator reviews and signs off; the human owns the decision. Click through a before-and-after process map and run it live.
home.workflow.heading: See the automation run, on synthetic data.

notes.index.sub: Short essays on the design of personal AI infrastructure. Concepts I keep coming back to. Linked from the rest of the site where the concept first appears in context.
projects.index.card.devswarm.body: A FastMCP server that orchestrates five persistent Claude Code persona sessions (architect, researcher, frontend, backend, reviewer-deployer) to turn one idea into a working pull request. Fire-and-forget; no Docker, no daemon, no port. Just files on disk and stdio MCP.
projects.index.card.devswarm.label: from one prompt to one PR, end-to-end
projects.index.card.devswarm.title: DevSwarm v1
projects.index.card.exocortex.body: A personal AI assistant accessible through Even Realities G2 smart glasses. Voice from the lenses, pipeline through self-hosted infrastructure on Fedora, all routed through Claude Max OAuth so there's no per-token API billing. Multilingual: English, German, Russian, French.
projects.index.card.exocortex.label: day 5 · base process complete
projects.index.card.exocortex.title: Personal AI Exocortex
projects.index.sub: Small experiments in agent design, knowledge tooling, and the workflow plumbing between them. Built for myself first, written up here when something works. Each card opens a longer write-up: architecture, decisions, and the artifacts the build produced.
